label: Data Abstraction
description: The process of hiding irrelevant details from the user and showing only necessary information.

label: Data Independence
description: Users and data should not directly interact with each other.

label: Levels of Data Abstraction
description: Total three levels in database management system: View level, conceptual level, and physical level.

label: Physical Level (Internal Schema)
description: The lowest level of data abstraction, defining how data are stored and its location in memory, completely handled by the database administrator.

label: Conceptual Level (Logical Level)
description: The level that defines what data are stored in the database, relationships among data, and the structure of the entire database, handled by DBA, programmer, or developer.

label: View Level (External Schema)
description: The level that tells the application how data should be shown to the user, providing multiple views of the same database for user interaction.

label: Database Management System (DBMS)
description: A system where data abstraction and data independence are applied and managed.

label: Database Administrator (DBA)
description: An entity responsible for deciding data storage, arrangement, and managing the physical and conceptual levels of data abstraction.

label: User
description: An entity that accesses the system, shown only necessary information, and directly interacts with the view level.

label: 3 Tier Architecture
description: A database architecture discussed in the last presentation.

label: View of Data
description: The main focus of the current presentation.

label: Multiple Views
description: Different perspectives of data existing at the presentation level.

label: Primary Goal of Database
description: To store and retrieve data conveniently and effectively.

label: Storage
description: Required for storing and retrieving data, potentially involving simple or complex data structures.

label: Hiding Complexity
description: A core principle of data abstraction.

label: Essential Details
description: The desired outcome from data abstraction, providing a short and sweet answer.

label: Levels of Abstraction
description: Several layers used to achieve data abstraction.

label: Physical Level
description: The lowest level of data abstraction.

label: Logical Level
description: The middle level of data abstraction, dealing with what data are stored and their relationships.

label: View Level
description: The topmost and highest level of data abstraction, interacting directly with users.

label: How Data Stored
description: The concern of the physical level, dealing with how data are stored.

label: Data Structures (Physical Level)
description: Data structures used at the physical level, which can be low-level, simple or complex.

label: What Data Stored
description: The concern of the logical level, dealing with what data are stored.

label: Relationship Among Data
description: The relationship among data stored in the database, described at the logical level.

label: Data Structures (Logical Level)
description: Simple data structures used at the logical level.

label: Physical Data Independence
description: A concept where applications do not depend on the physical level or physical schema, allowing changes in logical structure without affecting physical structure.

label: Schema
description: The structure of the database.

label: User Interaction
description: Interaction with the system, done at the view level.

label: Interfaces
description: Means by which users interact with the system, such as web, mobile, or graphical user interfaces (GUI).

label: Security
description: An important aspect achieved at the view level by providing restricted access to data for users.

label: Application Level
description: Another name for the logical level.
